blockers: The fallback accepts the entire archive as soon as any one member verifies, leaving every other regular file unverified; this is not a safe archive-level trust decision.
Only the first 20 filesystem-discovered files are considered, so archive ordering and directory iteration can silently place unverified payloads outside the verification set.
Symlinks and non-regular archive entries are skipped rather than causing rejection, and file-collection errors are discarded, so malformed or mixed-entry archives do not fail closed.
overall_reasoning: The agent investigated the existing GitHub attestation bridge and both lock-time and install-time call paths, preserved archive-blob verification as the first choice, and ultimately ran focused crate tests plus the main package suite. However, it did not ask the consequential coverage-policy question despite recognizing the ambiguity, then chose the permissive rule that one verified member authenticates an entire archive. The 20-file truncation, skipped entry types, discarded collection errors, and suppression of per-member verification errors compound that trust-boundary flaw. The deterministic verifier also rejects the implementation because the GitHub backend never reaches a semantically complete archive-aware verifier. This is a valid behavioral failure, not an infrastructure issue, and the review does not override it.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The archive-first control flow and helpers are understandable, but the 600-line addition duplicates archive handling in the sigstore crate and encodes important security policy through ad hoc booleans, truncation, and continue branches.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The change uses the shared GitHub attestation bridge, but adds seven archive dependencies and a second extraction implementation to a low-level verification crate instead of integrating with the repository's established archive and SLSA subject abstractions.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes are limited to mise-sigstore and its lockfile, but the implementation is disproportionately large and broadens behavior for every caller without establishing that all those call paths should use this archive fallback.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Archive-level verification remains preferred and existing invalid archive attestations do not downgrade, but one-of-N acceptance, a 20-file cap, skipped special entries, ignored traversal/collection outcomes, dropped signer-workflow policy, and swallowed member verification errors are fail-open choices at the archive trust boundary.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The tests cover format detection, extraction, cleanup, digests, and two negative API paths, and the agent ran both focused and broad suites. They do not prove a valid end-to-end member attestation, complete-member coverage, more-than-20-member safety, hard links, duplicate paths, traversal rejection, collection errors, or preservation of signer policy; some tests explicitly lock in unsafe skipping and truncation behavior.

strengths: Traced the shared sigstore bridge and relevant lock-time and install-time verification paths before editing.
Preserved valid archive-blob attestation verification as the preferred path and did not downgrade after an existing archive attestation failed.
Added focused extraction and negative-path tests, then ran crate tests, clippy, formatting, and the main package test suite.
summary: Thorough investigation and useful local verification, but the fallback is unmergeable because it authenticates an entire archive from any one of at most 20 members and skips or ignores other trust-boundary conditions.
